key_insight: The paper shows that agentic AI systems can help build a machine-checked Lean formalization of Shor’s algorithm and its resource estimates for RSA-2048 and P-256.
engineering_relevance: This advances trustworthy quantum cryptanalysis by turning high-level attack analyses into verifiable proofs that can support future secure hardware and post-quantum security studies.
method: Used an agentic workflow where software agents searched sources, wrote Lean code, and repaired proofs.
Formalized the mathematical foundations of Shor-style order finding plus reversible quantum circuits for modular and elliptic-curve arithmetic.
Derived logical resource estimates for RSA-2048 and P-256 and added classical operation estimates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports machine-checked Lean formal proofs, with human review of the scientific claims, for resource-estimate analyses based on prior quantum cryptography results.
watch_item: The formalization is proof-level and model-dependent, so practical quantum resource costs may still differ from the abstract estimates if circuit assumptions or implementation details change.
paper_title: Building Shor's Algorithm in Lean: An Agentic Formalization of Quantum Attacks on RSA-2048 and P-256
